Off Calendar Apr-21-2022 Continued To Sep-12-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Defendant For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT ALEXA NEDELLEC's Motion Of Defendant For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Matter is transferred to be heard on the discovery calendar, Department 301, at 9:00 a.m. on the same day.

Judge: Suzanne R. Bolanos; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported (302/SRB)

Defendant Alexa Nedellec'S Motion Of Defendant For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff
Off Calendar Apr-15-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Daniel Musni (415.794.9351) Plaintiff in propria persona (audio only).
Alexander MacDougall, Esq. (925.825.9500; almacdougall@geico.com) of Law Offices of Catherine A. Walsh for Defendant.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

Defendant Alexa Nedellec's Motion For Terminating Sanctions Against Plaintiff:

The motion for terminating sanctions is DENIED. This ruling is made without prejudice to the Defendant filing a motion to compel attendance at deposition and/or a motion to compel responses to written discovery. (Dep't of Forestry & Fire Prot. v. Howell (2017) 18 Cal.App.5th 154, 191-92 ["[S]anctions are generally imposed in an incremental approach, with terminating sanctions being the last resort."].) The request for monetary sanctions is DENIED. Counsel's declaration failed to set forth any facts or evidence regarding the cost(s) associated with the deposition at which the Plaintiff failed to appear.

The court will prepare an order.

Judge Pro Tem: Noah Lebowitz, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
